mechanism_summary: Hamming gradient in CMP_MAP rewards each bit-step of the 32-bit codepoint toward 0x0A85, preserving intermediate seeds; cmplog has no such distance signal.
one_line: VP gradient climbs 32-bit codepoint toward Gujarati literal 0x0A85

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance feedback rewards each upward step of the 1-byte bit_depth field toward threshold 16, giving a monotone gradient that cmplog's unanchored I2S substitution lacks.
one_line: VP gradient walks 1-byte bit_depth field up to threshold 16

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards intermediate parsing progress through chained upstream string comparisons, driving corpus 10x-70x deeper into the XML parsing pipeline than cmplog's unguided I2S substitution reaches.
one_line: VP gradient climbs multi-step XML parse chain to reach error enum arm

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming-distance bucket preserves seeds whose 32-bit first word gets numerically closer to 0x0D91, yielding a monotone gradient naive's edge-only feedback cannot construct.
one_line: VP gradient walks 4-byte input word toward Sinhala codepoint 0x0D91

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP records per-character proximity buckets at every single-byte state-transition gate in the DOCTYPE state machine, building a gradient through the chained character comparisons that naive's edge-only counter cannot signal.
one_line: VP gradient navigates chained single-byte state-machine gates toward bracket sequence

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming-distance feedback rewards bytes at the scanned offset that are bit-closer to 0x5D, building a corpus gradient toward the ']' match that naive's binary edge feedback cannot provide.
one_line: VP gradient rewards bit-proximity to literal 0x5D at scanned offset

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient distinguishes the bitwise-AND result against 0x02 bit, rewarding inputs that flip the color_type bitmask bit toward zero; cmplog's I2S cannot treat a 1-bit mask as a substitutable magic constant.
one_line: VP gradient flips color_type bitmask bit 0x02 toward zero

mechanism_summary: VP gradient rewards upstream comparisons leading to MODI script selection; cmplog's I2S substitutes competing script codepoint literals, steering the derived script field away from MODI and polluting the corpus.
one_line: VP gradient escapes I2S corpus pollution toward derived MODI script value

mechanism_summary: VP Hamming-distance feedback rewards progress on allocation-size comparisons independently of I2S substitution; cmplog's I2S pulls the 4-byte iTXt length field back toward plausible recorded values, undoing large-length mutations that trigger the NULL allocation path.
one_line: VP gradient preserves oversized iTXt length that I2S substitution reverts
